origin.name: Namibia
origin.death_registration: Ministry of Home Affairs and Immigration
origin.key_document: Death certificate from Ministry of Home Affairs (English language)
origin.source: FCDO Namibia travel advice, updated 2025; British High Commission Windhoek guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Namibia is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is an option alongside full body repatriation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Death certificate from Ministry of Home Affairs (English language)
special_rules: Namibia issues death certificates in English, which simplifies UK documentation requirements.
Police clearance from the Namibian Police Force is required for all unnatural or suspicious deaths.
An export permit from Namibian authorities is required for all repatriations.
Embalming must be carried out by a licensed funeral director before air transport.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. Namibian documentation is in English. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British High Commission in Windhoek can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Namibia to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Namibia. Repatriation to the UK takes 2-3 weeks. Ministry of Home Affairs, police clearance, and export permits all handled. Contact us 24/7.
